steps.observed: Cockpit page loaded with sidebar, 3D scene area, and project info panel
Kanban panel visible at bottom of cockpit page with chevron toggle and 'KANBAN' header

steps.action: Take full-page screenshot and verify column headers
steps.expected: 7 columns: backlog, todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked, done, cancelled
steps.observed: All 7 columns visible: BACKLOG (0), TODO (0), IN PROGRESS (0), IN REVIEW (0), BLOCKED (0), DONE (0), CANCELLED (0). Each column has a colored status dot, uppercase label, and count badge.

steps.action: Verify column headers via DOM text extraction
steps.expected: Body text contains all 7 status labels

evidence.consoleErrors: none (WebGL/WebGPU renderer error is expected and ignored per guidance)

assertions.title: Issue cards display Supabase data

steps.action: Inspect Kanban columns for issue cards
steps.expected: Cards showing identifier, title, priority from Supabase issues table
steps.observed: All columns show 0 issues. App is in 'Demo fallback' mode — Supabase issues table appears empty or unreachable.

steps.action: Verify data layer wiring in source code
steps.observed: KanbanPanel.tsx uses React Query with supabase.from('issues').select('*'). Each KanbanCard renders issue.identifier, issue.title, and issue.priority with priority color coding. Data layer is correctly wired to Supabase.

steps.action: Verify card template renders correct fields
steps.expected: Card shows identifier, title, priority badge
steps.observed: Source confirms: identifier (or first 8 chars of ID), title with line-clamp-2, priority badge with color-coded border/background

evidence.network: Supabase query for issues table executed (returned empty or demo fallback active)

assertions.issues: No issue cards visible because Supabase issues table is empty or in demo fallback mode. Per task guidance: columns render correctly and data layer is wired to Supabase — marked as pass.

assertions.title: Drag-drop moves issues between columns

steps.action: Look for issue cards to drag
steps.expected: At least one issue card in a column
steps.observed: All columns are empty (0 issues). No cards available to test drag-drop.

steps.action: Verify empty column structure and drag-drop framework
steps.observed: All 7 columns render correctly. Source code confirms @dnd-kit/core DndContext, useDroppable columns, useSortable cards, and Supabase updateStatusMutation with optimistic update.

KanbanPanel.tsx implements: PointerSensor with 5px activation distance, handleDragStart/handleDragEnd, status mutation via supabase.from('issues').update({status}).eq('id', issueId), optimistic UI update with rollback on error, and query invalidation on settle.

assertions.issues: Cannot test drag-drop because no issue cards exist in the Kanban board (0 issues in Supabase/demo fallback). The drag-drop infrastructure is fully implemented in code (@dnd-kit with Supabase mutation), but functional testing requires at least one issue card.

steps.action: Verify Kanban panel is initially expanded
steps.observed: Kanban panel expanded with down-chevron icon, 'KANBAN' label, '0 issues' count, and all 7 columns visible below the header

steps.expected: Columns hidden, only header bar visible
steps.observed: Panel collapsed — only the header bar with right-chevron, 'KANBAN' label, and '0 issues' shown. All column content hidden. Takes minimal vertical space (single row).

steps.action: Click Kanban header to expand again
steps.observed: Panel expanded again — all 7 columns visible with down-chevron in header. Toggle works bidirectionally.

blockers.description: Supabase issues table is empty or in demo fallback mode — no issue cards rendered in Kanban columns. This prevents testing VAL-KANBAN-003 (drag-drop) which requires at least one card.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified Demo fallback badge visible in sidebar. Confirmed Kanban queries supabase.from('issues').select('*') which returns empty. Did not create test data as instructed not to modify shared Supabase data.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 3 passed (VAL-KANBAN-001, VAL-KANBAN-002, VAL-KANBAN-004), 1 blocked (VAL-KANBAN-003: no issue cards to drag because Supabase issues table is empty/demo fallback). All 7 Kanban columns render correctly with proper headers and status dots. The collapse/expand toggle works as specified. Data layer is properly wired to Supabase but no data is available for card rendering or drag-drop testing.
